conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility criteria.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. I've reviewed the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have one child, Alex, who is 18 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to know a bit more about their current situation.

Is Alex currently in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment. They finished school but haven't gone on to do anything else.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I have a few more questions to help determine your eligibility.

When did Alex finish their education or training - do you know the specific date or roughly when they left?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: It was a while ago now - more than a few months back. They haven't been in education for quite some time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. I have one more important question:

Has Alex started any paid work, and if so, are they working 24 hours or more per week?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex hasn't started any paid work. They're not working at all at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I have one final question to complete my assessment:

Is Alex receiving any benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't receiving any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for answering all my questions. I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the information you've provided and the official UK government rules, I need to inform you of my decision:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Assessment Result: Not Eligible**

I'm sorry to inform you that you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Here's why:**

For children under 16, you automatically qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for them. However, for young people aged 16 or over, they must be both:
- Under 20 years old, **AND**
- In approved education or training

While Alex is under 20 (at 18 years old) and lives with you, they are not currently in approved education or training and haven't been for several months. This means the eligibility criteria are not met.

**What could change this:**

If Alex were to enroll in approved education or training in the future, you could then apply for Child Benefit again, as long as they remain under 20 years old.

If you have any questions about what counts as approved education or training, or if Alex's circumstances change, you can contact the Child Benefit Office for further guidance.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and is not in approved education or training. According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify if your child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 18 and not currently in approved education or training, and left education more than a few months ago, they do not meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. Alex is not working 24 hours or more per week and is not receiving benefits in their own right, but these factors do not make them eligible - the key issue is that at 18 years old, they must be in approved education or training to qualify for Child Benefit, which they are not.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, aged 18, who lives at home. Alex is not in approved education or training and has not been for several months. While Alex is not working or claiming benefits, the fundamental requirement for Child Benefit for anyone aged 16 or over is that they must be under 20 AND in approved education or training. Since Alex does not meet this requirement, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

While Alex is not working or claiming benefits, the fundamental requirement for Child Benefit for anyone aged 16 or over is that they must be under 20 AND in approved education or training. Since Alex does not meet this requirement, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
